steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage moves from a broad statement ('Tacettin Diker is well known for his work as a puppeteer') to a specific detail ('he specializes in shadow puppetry'). The transition must signal that the second sentence narrows down or specifies what the first sentence said — not contrast, cause-effect, or enumeration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In particular,': Signals that the second sentence zeroes in on a specific type within the broader category just named. Shadow puppetry is a particular kind of puppetry — exactly the right relationship. ✓
  • Choice B — 'By contrast,': Implies the second sentence opposes the first. Specializing in shadow puppetry doesn't oppose being a puppeteer. ✗
  • Choice C — 'By doing so,': Implies the second sentence is a result or consequence of an action in the first. No action is performed in sentence one. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Second,': Implies this is the second item in a list. There is no first item. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
